payload.title: 2010-2014 Capital Program Amendment
payload.description: Proposed amendment to the MTA 2010-2014 Capital Program reducing the overall program from $31.704 billion to $31.561 billion, including Superstorm Sandy budget adjustments, B&T envelope reductions, and project timing modifications.

tool_args.label: New Coney Island Yard Complex Sandy Project

payload.description: New grant funded project to address Superstorm Sandy damage at the Coney Island Complex
Unused program reserves released from Sandy portion of B&T program
Program within the 2010-2014 Capital Program with $3,192.9 million budget. Remaining rolling stock reserves of $463 million for future M9A growth fleet for LIRR.

tool_args.label: Coney Island Yard Complex Superstorm Sandy Repair

payload.project_name: Coney Island Yard Complex Superstorm Sandy Repair
payload.status: In development, not expected to be awarded before 2025
payload.description: New $224 million grant funded project to address Superstorm Sandy damage at the Coney Island Complex, leveraging savings within NYCT's Sandy restoration program

warnings: project.program is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Program names caused identity overmerge; they are relation context, not strong project identity. Suggested relation: part_of_program.

payload.claim_text: Since the September 2019 Amendment, there have been considerable changes to the Sandy program reflecting actual estimates at award and completion. NYCT has leveraged savings within its Sandy restoration program to create a new $224 million project that will address storm damage at its Coney Island Yard Complex.
payload.subject: Superstorm Sandy program

payload.claim_text: MTA Bridge and Tunnels 2010-2014 Capital Program is 96% complete as of this amendment and the remaining 4% of project budgets are in financial closeout.
payload.subject: MTA Bridges and Tunnels 2010-2014 Capital Program
payload.description: Program nearing completion

payload.claim_text: The Signals and Communications category increase by a total of +$60 million due to an increase in the Signal Modernization element +$104 million, which was partially offset by a decrease in the Communications Systems element (-$44 million).
payload.subject: NYCT Signals and Communications category

payload.claim_text: The Passenger Stations category decreases (-$101 million) due to select projects that transferred scope to the 2015-2019 Program, resulting in budget decreases in the 2010-2014 Capital Program and corresponding increases in the 2015-2019 Capital Program.
payload.subject: NYCT Passenger Stations category

payload.description: Overall budget remains unchanged since the last amendment

tool_args.label: East Side Access Rolling Stock Reserve

payload.description: Rolling stock reserves remaining to be awarded for East Side Access that will support a future M9A growth fleet for the LIRR
MTA Board approved proposed $23.812 billion 2010-2014 Capital Program

tool_args.label: Proposed Amendment to MTA Board - October 30, 2024

payload.description: 2010-2014 Capital Program Amendment No. 7 As Proposed to the MTA Board
Superstorm Sandy struck the region, leading to restoration and mitigation projects in the capital program
MTA Board approved amendment reducing overall capital program from $32.021 billion to $31.704 billion

tool_args.label: CPRB Agency Elements with Increases Exceeding 10%

payload.claim_text: Pursuant to the Public Authorities Law, changes to elements that are greater than 10% require approval of the CPRB to be progressed. Elements identified include NYCT T61602 Miscellaneous ($223.6M to $289.2M), NYCT ET1603 Sandy Miscellaneous Mitigation ($83.0M to $116.0M), NYCT ET1002 Sandy Shops & Yards Restoration ($924.1M to $1,025.0M), and LIRR L60601 LIRR Shops & Yards ($112.4M to $125.2M).
